principle: Every link text must let users predict what they will find at the destination — vague labels like 'Learn more' or 'Click here' have zero information scent and turn each click into a blind commitment
explanation: X.ux|link_scent: information_scent==user_confidence_that_click_leads_toward_goal; strong_scent: specific_destination_description -> user_knows_exactly_what_awaits; zero_scent: action_verbs_without_object ("click_here" + "learn_more" + "read_more" + "find_out"); weak_scent: destination_category_only ("products" + "services"); strong_scent_formula: [what_user_gets]+[qualifier] eg: "Download_the_pricing_guide_PDF" NOT "Learn_more"; applies_to: nav_links+inline_body_links+CTAs+search_result_titles; NO:"learn_more"_as_CTA NO:"click_here"_as_link_text NO:CTA_describing_action_only_not_destination; eg: "Learn_more" >> "Read_the_2025_pricing_breakdown" OR "Get_30_days_free"
